summary: Surah Ya-Sin highlights the Qur’an’s wisdom and the mission of Prophet Muhammad as a mercy and warner. It recounts the story of the people of the city who rejected messengers and were destroyed, and emphasizes Allah’s signs in creation. The surah stresses the reality of resurrection, the consequences of belief and disbelief, the fate of Paradise and Hell, and the power of Allah’s command. It reminds of gratitude, warns against arrogance, and reassures the Prophet against rejection.
